units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच परं भूय: प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् । ‍‍ ॥ यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनय: सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गता: ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | paraṁ bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi jñānānāṁ jñānam uttamam | yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṁ siddhim ito gatāḥ || 1 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: I shall speak again of the supreme Knowledge, the best of all knowledge, by realizing which all the contemplatives reached the highest Perfection from here.

units.sanskrit: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागता: । सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: idaṁ jñānam upāśritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ | sarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti ca || 2 ||
units.translation: Those who attain identity with Me by resorting to this Knowledge are not born even during creation, nor do they suffer pain during dissolution.

units.sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्‍ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् । सम्भव: सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham | sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata || 3 ||
units.translation: My womb is the great-sustainer. In that I place the seed. From that, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, occurs the birth of all things.

units.sanskrit: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तय: सम्भवन्ति या: । तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रद: पिता ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-yoniṣu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ | tāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā || 4 ||
units.translation: O son of Kuntī, whatever forms are born from all the wombs, of them the great-sustainer is the womb; I am the father who deposits the seed.

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणा: प्रकृतिसम्भवा: । निबध्‍नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛti-sambhavāḥ | nibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam || 5 ||
units.translation: O mighty-armed one, the guṇas, viz. sattva, rajas and  tamas, born of Nature, being the immutable embodied being to the body.

units.sanskrit: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् । सुखसङ्गेन बध्‍नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśakam anāmayam | sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena cānagha || 6 ||
units.translation: Among them, sattva, being pure, is an illuminator and is harmless. O sinless one, it binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.

units.sanskrit: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भ‍वम् । तन्निबध्‍नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛṣṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam | tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam || 7 ||
units.translation: Know rajas to be of the nature of passion, born of hankering and attachment. O son of Kuntī, that binds the embodied one through attachment to action.

units.sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् । प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्‍नाति भारत ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām | pramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata || 8 ||
units.translation: On the other hand, know tamas, which deludes all embodied beings, to be born of ignorance. O scion of the Bharata dynasty, that binds through inadvertence, laziness and sleep.

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रज: कर्मणि भारत । ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तम: प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata | jñānam āvṛtya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta || 9 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, sattva attaches one to happiness, rajas to action, while tamas, covering up knowledge, leads to inadvertence also.

units.sanskrit: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत । रज: सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तम: सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: rajas tamaś cābhibhūya sattvaṁ bhavati bhārata | rajaḥ sattvaṁ tamaś caiva tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajas tathā || 10 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, sattva increases by subduing rajas and tamas, rajas by overpowering sattva and tamas, and tamas by dominating over sattva and rajas.

units.sanskrit: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते । ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-dvāreṣu dehe ’smin prakāśa upajāyate | jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛddhaṁ sattvam ity uta || 11 ||
units.translation: When the illumination that is knowledge radiates in this body through all the doors (of the senses), then one should know that sattva has increased greatly.

units.sanskrit: लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा । रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: lobhaḥ pravṛttir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśamaḥ spṛhā | rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe bharatarṣabha || 12 ||
units.translation: O best of the Bhārata dynasty, when rajas becomes predominant, these come into being: avarice, movement, undertaking of actions, unrest and hankering.

units.sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च । तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: aprakāśo ’pravṛttiś ca pramādo moha eva ca | tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe kuru-nandana || 13 ||
units.translation: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty, when tamas predominates these surely come into being: non-discrimination and inactivity, inadvertence and delusion.

units.sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् । तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā sattve pravṛddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛt | tadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyate || 14 ||
units.translation: When an embodied one undergoes death while sattva is exclusively predominant, then he attains the taintless worlds of those who know the highest (truth).

units.sanskrit: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते । तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: rajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣu jāyate | tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣu jāyate || 15 ||
units.translation: When one dies while rajas predominates, he is born among people attached to activity. Similarly, when one dies while tamas predominates, he takes birth among the stupid species.

units.sanskrit: कर्मण: सुकृतस्याहु: सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् । रजसस्तु फलं दु:खमज्ञानं तमस: फलम् ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: karmaṇaḥ sukṛtasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṁ nirmalaṁ phalam | rajasas tu phalaṁ duḥkham ajñānaṁ tamasaḥ phalam || 16 ||
units.translation: They say that the result of good work is pure and is born of sattva. But the result of rajas is sorrow; the result of tamas is ignorance.

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च । प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṁ rajaso lobha eva ca | pramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva ca || 17 ||
units.translation: From sattva is born knowledge, and from rajas, verily, avarice. From tamas are born inadvertence and delusion as also ignorance, to be sure.

units.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसा: । जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसा: ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ gacchanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ | jaghanya-guṇa-vṛtti-sthā adho gacchanti tāmasāḥ || 18 ||
units.translation: People who conform to sattva go higher up; those who conform to rajas stay in the middle; those who conform to tamas, who conform to the actions of the lowest quality, go down.

units.sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्य: कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति । गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भ‍ावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati | guṇebhyaś ca paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so ’dhigacchati || 19 ||
units.translation: When the witness sees none other than the guṇas as the agent, and knows that which is superior to the guṇas, he attains My nature.

units.sanskrit: गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भ‍वान् । जन्ममृत्युजरादु:खैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्न‍ुते ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavān | janma-mṛtyu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛtam aśnute || 20 ||
units.translation: Having transcended these three guṇas which are the origin of the body, the embodied one, becoming free from birth, death, old age and sorrows, experiences Immortality.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो । किमाचार: कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho | kim-ācāraḥ kathaṁ caitāṁs trīn guṇān ativartate || 21 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: O Bhagavān, by what signs is one (known) who has gone beyond these three guṇas? What is his behaviour, and how does he transcend these three guṇas?

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव । न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्‍क्षति ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | prakāśaṁ ca pravṛttiṁ ca moham eva ca pāṇḍava | na dveṣṭi sampravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati || 22 ||
units.translation: O son of Pandu, he neither dislikes illumination (knowledge), activity and delusion when they appear, nor does he long for them when they disappear.

units.sanskrit: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते । गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: udāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vicālyate | guṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣṭhati neṅgate || 23 ||
units.translation: He who, sitting like one indifferent, is not distracted by the three guṇas; he who, thinking that the guṇas alone act, remains firm and surely does not move;

units.sanskrit: समदु:खसुख: स्वस्थ: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन: । तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुति: ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣṭāśma-kāñcanaḥ | tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-saṁstutiḥ || 24 ||
units.translation: He to whom sorrow and happiness are alike, who is established in his own Self, to whom a lump of earth, iron and gold are the same, to whom the agreeable and the disagreeable are the same, who is wise, to whom censure and his own praise are the same;

units.sanskrit: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयो: । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीत: स उच्यते ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣayoḥ | sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa ucyate || 25 ||
units.translation: He who is the same under honour and dishonour, who is equally disposed both towards the side of the friend and of the foe, who has renounced all enterprise—he is said to have gone beyond the guṇas.

units.sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते । स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: māṁ ca yo ’vyabhicāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate | sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate || 26 ||
units.translation: And he who serves Me through the unswerving Yoga of Devotion, he, having gone beyond these guṇas, qualifies for becoming Brahman.

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च । शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāhamn amṛtasyāvyayasya ca | śāśvatasya ca dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya ca || 27 ||
units.translation: For I am the Abode of Brahman—the indestructible and immutable, the eternal, the Dharma and absolute Bliss.
